content: Hi, I'm Spencer. I just got back from a trip to the UK and I'm still recovering from the jet lag. I spent 10 days traveling through London, Manchester, and Edinburgh.
Welcome back, Spencer! The UK is such a great destination. What were some of the highlights of your trip?
Definitely the board game cafes I visited in London. I'm a big fan of board games, and it was great to meet other gamers and try out some new games.
That sounds like a lot of fun. Board game cafes are such a great way to meet new people and have a good time. Did you try out any new games during your trip?
Yes, I did. I tried out a game called Gloomhaven, which was really interesting. And I also got to visit some amazing museums and galleries, which was really great for me as a podcast listener.
That's terrific! Gloomhaven is a great game. And museums and galleries can be such a great way to learn about a culture. Did you have a favorite city or town that you visited?
I think my favorite was Edinburgh. The people were so friendly and the food was amazing. I ended up spending 3 days there and even got to visit the National Museum of Scotland on August 10th.

Edinburgh is a beautiful city. The culture and history are so rich. And visiting the National Museum of Scotland must have been a great experience. How did you find the food in the UK compared to what you're used to in the US?
I loved it. I'm a big fan of fish and chips, and I got to try all sorts of local specialties. I even took a knitting class in Manchester where I learned how to make traditional British scarves.
That sounds like a great experience. Knitting can be such a relaxing hobby. Did you have a favorite dish that you tried during your trip?
Definitely the fish and chips. I've already made it a few times since I got back. And I'm thinking of entering a knitting competition in my hometown of Denver next year.
That's great! It's always exciting to share your passions with others. Good luck with the competition, and I hope you get to share your love of knitting with the community.

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. And I'm also thinking of starting a blog to write about my travel experiences and board game adventures. Maybe I'll even get to write about my next trip, which I'm planning for January 2025.
